coding_notes: Replicate 3 verdicts. Anthropic r3 again called out the glycosylation tradeoff (C6.3 affirm, stable across r1+r2+r3). OpenAI r3 shifted from split-finding (r1+r2) to MORE-benefit direction (C6.1 affirm, DRIFT). DeepSeek r3 picked chaperone-competition tradeoff (C6.2 ignore - drift from r1 affirm).

claims.claim_text: Q141K carriers benefit MORE from the uricase + lactoferrin combination than wild-type ABCG2 (compounding-deficit logic).

deepseek.value_quoted: MORE benefit via enterocyte pyroptosis suppression

openai.value_quoted: ACTUAL r3: 'Q141K carriers may benefit more from a uricase + lactoferrin combination' - DRIFT from r1+r2 split-finding disagree

anthropic.value_quoted: ACTUAL r3: 'disproportionately greater anti-inflammatory benefit per unit of co-administered lactoferrin'

claims.claim_text: Single-strain co-expression locks the cassette dose ratio, which conflicts with genotype-stratified dosing.

deepseek.value_quoted: r3 picked chaperone-competition tradeoff, not fixed-ratio

openai.value_quoted: ACTUAL r3: 'uricase activity units, not total secreted protein, the dominant design constraint'

anthropic.value_quoted: ACTUAL r3: picked glycan-budget tradeoff, not fixed-ratio

claims.claim_text: Glycosylation differences between gut-luminal vs systemic action create a payload-incompatibility (uricase wants minimal glycan, lactoferrin wants native-like).

anthropic.value_quoted: ACTUAL r3: 'glycan-budget constraint' uricase wants stability glycan, lactoferrin wants receptor glycan - stable across r1+r2+r3

claims.claim_text: Includes a testable in-silico or low-cost hypothesis to discriminate between proposed mechanisms.
